article_title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen to Step Down After 18 Years as AI Pressure and Stock Decline Reshape the Creative Software Giant

content_preview.title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen to Step Down After 18 Years as AI Pressure and Stock Decline Reshape the Creative Software Giant
content_preview.summary: Narayen will remain as board chair while Adobe searches for a successor amid a stock slide from $688 to under $250 and mounting AI competition.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Shantanu Narayen, the chief executive who transformed Adobe from a boxed-software maker into a subscription-driven cloud giant, announced on March 12 that he will step down once a successor is appointed. In a [statement to Adobe's board](https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/03/leadership-update), Narayen said he will remain as chair to support the transition, mirroring the arrangement under which Adobe's co-founders supported his own appointment in 2007.

recommendations: Consider adding news.adobe.com and petapixel.com to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 760 words (within 400-1200 range). Clear sections: Overview, What We Know, A Divided Legacy, What We Don't Know, Analysis. Professional tone throughout with appropriate technical depth for a leadership transition story.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources fetched and verified. (1) news.adobe.com/leadership-update: confirms Narayen stepping down, remaining as chair, Frank Calderoni leading successor search committee, internal/external candidates considered. (2) news.adobe.com/employee-memo: confirms 28-year career, growth from 3,000 to 30,000+ employees, revenue from under $1B to over $25B, AI/creativity quotes verified verbatim. (3) Fortune: confirms Q1 FY2026 revenue of $6.4B (12.1% YoY), AI product revenue tripled YoY, record cash flow of $2.96B, Q2 guidance of $6.43-6.48B, Satya Nadella 'empathy' quote. (4) PetaPixel: confirms divided legacy framing, subscription model criticism from creatives, Firefly 'commercially safe' positioning and subsequent strategy shift, competitor gains (DaVinci Resolve, Affinity, Apple Creator Studio, Canva), stock trajectory from ~$40 to $688 peak.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All financial figures, quotes, and factual claims verified against cited sources. Stock decline from $688 peak to under $250 is consistent with PetaPixel reporting $244. Employee and revenue growth figures match both Adobe's memo and Fortune's reporting. No hallucinated quotes or misattributed claims detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The 'Divided Legacy' section fairly presents both the investor and creative community perspectives without editorializing. No sensationalism in the headline despite the dramatic stock decline angle. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: No existing articles on Adobe CEO transition in the archive. Fresh topic covering a breaking leadership change.
recommendations: Consider adding news.adobe.com (Adobe's official newsroom, primary source) and petapixel.com (established photography/creative tech publication) to the source allowlist, as both are reputable sources appropriate for this type of story.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission with strong sourcing. All four sources verified and claims accurately attributed. The article provides balanced coverage of a significant corporate leadership transition, contextualizing it within the broader AI disruption narrative. Ready for publication.
